content: Self-Refine’s task-focused iterative output polishing and Reflexion’s dynamic memory-backed self-reflection can be combined into a two-layer autonomous improvement framework: Self-Refine handles incremental refinement of immediate task outputs, while Reflexion accumulates cross-task reflection patterns to fix systematic flaws that repeated single-task self-feedback cannot catch. This framework retains both methods’ core advantage of no extra supervised training or reinforcement learning, while solving the common limitation that both approaches fail to correct persistent blind spots across repeated task attempts.
